differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus with Libman-Sacks endocarditis
Disseminated malignancy with non-bacterial thrombotic endocarditis
next_steps: Antinuclear antibody (ANA) and antiphospholipid antibody panel
Blood culture and specialized culture (e.g., Bartonella, HACEK organisms)
Consultation with rheumatology and infectious disease
Screening for underlying malignancy (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis already done; consider peripheral blood smear evaluation for schistocytes)
Cerebral angiography if suspecting vasculitis after infection ruled out

differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus with Libman-Sacks endocarditis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
next_steps: Transesophageal echocardiography to visualize valvular vegetations
Blood cultures for fastidious organisms (Bartonella, HACEK, Coxiella)
Peripheral blood smear for schistocytes and LDH/haptoglobin to assess for MAHA
Antiphospholipid antibody panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta-2 glycoprotein)
Serum complement levels (C3, C4) and repeat autoimmune workup
Consultation with Infectious Disease and Rheumatology
Cardiac catheterization or vascular imaging to assess for mycotic aneurysms
